title: Point ogent-issues-bd.el at br (beads_rust): default executable, flag compatibility, tests
close_reason: ogent-issues-bd.el now defaults to br executable, sync uses --flush-only, error messages point to beads_rust; CLI flag surface verified compatible

title: Local Emacs 30.1 indent lint drift: 73 mismatches in armory/keys files (CI 30.2 green)
close_reason: Obsolete: upstream lint fixes (699858c) landed; fresh-sandbox make lint now exits 0 on local Emacs 30.1

title: Update AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / docs from bd+gt workflow to br workflow
close_reason: AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md rewritten for br workflow per bd-to-br-migration skill (verification greps pass); README/ROADMAP/docs/specs updated

title: Inline subtree asking: ogent-ask-here and ogent-ask-menu
description: Quick ask gains an in-buffer variant: ogent-ask-here inserts a folded Request and streams the Response under the subtree at point (request/response headlines now nest at the subtree's real level, response shifting is depth-aware, streamed text no longer merges into the following sibling heading); ogent-ask-menu names the active scope and offers inline ask, popup ask, context preview toggle, and the dispatcher. Ask scope climbs from bodyless/folded leaves to the nearest parent with visible content. Registry: q = ask-here, ? = ask-menu.
close_reason: Shipped: ogent-ask-here inserts folded Request + streamed Response at the subtree's real level (depth-aware shifting, sibling-heading merge fixed); ogent-ask-menu names the scope and routes to inline/popup ask, preview toggle, dispatcher; scope climbs from bodyless leaves. Implemented in the live session; verified (2504 tests) and committed here.

title: Fix lint: unknown-slot compile error and check-declare failures
close_reason: Duplicate of ogent-pag

title: Polish model picker: aliases, scope-aware unpin, browser origin, width-aware header
description: Post-merge polish round on the PR #6 picker driven by an interactive tmux/emacs -nw audit: official alias resolution (gpt-5.6, claude-haiku-4-5) canonicalized across get/roles/session/project/setters; @role typos no longer silently fall back; scope-aware unpin (direct vs inherited vs file, narrowing-safe); browser origin marker preserved across refresh/actions; picker roles header wraps at token boundaries using the transient window width.
close_reason: Landed: aliases canonicalized across all layers, scope-aware narrowing-safe unpin (incl. literal-dollar regexp fix), origin-preserving browser, width-aware wrapped roles header, @role typo guard; interactive tmux audit at 80/110/160 cols; 2663 tests 0 unexpected, lint exit 0

title: Cabinet: close the parity gaps
description: cabinet-parity.org promises behaviors that are partial or missing: a working scheduler, lead action parsing, conversation-aware search/graph, terminal runtime, session resume, and complete provider adapters.

title: Implement a scheduler that actually executes cron jobs and heartbeats
description: P1 (verified gap). cabinet-parity.org promises scheduled execution; ogent-cabinet-schedule.el computes schedules but nothing ever fires a cron job or heartbeat. Add the timer/execution layer.
close_reason: Emacs-timer scheduler shipped: ogent-cabinet-scheduler-mode + tick with arming semantics, catchup window, fired-slot dedup, per-root/per-event condition-case, auto-run gate; 7 tests + docs. Detached automations skipped (spec-optional)

title: Parse lead action proposals from run output into pending actions
description: P1 (gap). The lead workflow is supposed to turn run output into pending actions for approval, but proposals are never parsed out of the output. Implement the parse + pending-action store.
close_reason: Bead description was stale: parse/validate/store/UI already existed. Shipped the missing runner hook ogent-cabinet-runner--capture-actions wired into the sentinel; leads only, exit 0 only (failed-run partial output must not enqueue actions); unit + e2e tests

title: Include the canonical conversation store in Cabinet search
description: P1 (gap). Cabinet search excludes the canonical conversation store, so conversations are unsearchable from the search surface. Add it to the search corpus.
close_reason: Search corpus now appends .agents/.conversations index.org files via ogent-cabinet--conversation-org-files; ogent-cabinet-org-files and hidden-path regex untouched so the other four consumers are unaffected; kind filters work via existing metadata classification

title: Add conversations and parent/child task edges to the status graph
description: P2 (gap). ogent-cabinet-build-graph omits canonical conversation nodes and task parent/child edges that the spec graph model defines.

title: Make the terminal runtime mode actually run via a terminal
description: P2 (gap). 'terminal' runtime mode is advertised by adapters but no run ever executes in a terminal; it falls back to the pipe path. Implement or stop advertising it.

title: Implement adapter session resume instead of always replaying
description: P2 (gap). The continue/resume path always replays from scratch; adapters expose no real session-resume despite :supports-session-resume. Implement resume for the adapters that support it.

title: Add invocation builders for the Pi and Grok adapters
description: P2 (gap, acceptance criterion). The pi and grok adapters declare no :build-invocation, so they cannot run. Add builders or mark them unsupported.

title: Org-mode trifecta: compose with org, don't just store in it
description: The org-machinery census shows ogent mostly writes data into .org files rather than composing with org's engines (babel/agenda/capture/ql/export). The highest-leverage moves toward the emacs/org/LLM paragon live here.

title: ob-ogent: make prompts executable Org Babel src blocks with :var/:context and cached #+RESULTS
description: P1 (opportunity, the flagship org move). An ob-ogent babel backend turns a prompt into an executable src block: header args for model/preset/:context handles and :var bindings, C-c C-c runs it, output lands in cached #+RESULTS. This is the single highest-leverage step toward the trifecta paragon and reuses ogent-gptel.el + the context pipeline. org-babel call sites today: 0.
close_reason: Shipped ob-ogent: an Org Babel backend (org-babel-execute:ogent) that makes a prompt an executable src block. Header args :model/:system/:context/:var; ${name} placeholders expand from :var; @handles in :context resolve through ogent's context machinery and prepend; synchronous gptel-request returns the response into #+RESULTS. Registered with org-babel-load-languages on org load. Verified end-to-end: a real #+begin_src ogent block executed via org-babel-execute-src-block expands vars and lands the reply in #+RESULTS. 10 unit tests.

title: Register real Org TODO keywords for cabinet job/session states so org-agenda sees them
description: P1 (verified: org-mode leg). Cabinet job/session states are written as ad-hoc TODO keywords that are not registered, so org-agenda cannot collect them. Register the keyword set (and faces) and confirm cabinet records surface in agenda.
close_reason: Cabinet job/session states are now real Org TODO keywords. Added RUNNING (active) and FAILED (done-type) via ogent-cabinet-todo-keywords; ogent-cabinet-register-todo-keywords merges them into org-todo-keywords idempotently, deferred via with-eval-after-load (no require-time global mutation). Scaffolded index carries a self-describing #+TODO: line. Verified org-get-todo-state recognizes FAILED/RUNNING (not plain text), so org-agenda collects cabinet records. 3 new tests.

title: Project br beads into Org as agenda-visible TODOs beside the plan document
description: P2 (opportunity). Bridge br ready/blocked issues into org-agenda TODOs so beads and the plan live in one agenda. Reuses ogent-issues-bd.el (br JSON) + org-agenda.
close_reason: ogent-issues-agenda projects br issues into a generated org file (per-project cache under user-emacs-directory, never in worktree) with TODO/RUNNING/BLOCKED keywords, priorities, property drawers; agenda scoped + sticky; 10 tests; one-way by design

title: Build a Cabinet org-agenda custom command as the agent control plane
description: P2 (opportunity). A composite org-agenda custom command surfacing runs/retries/pending-actions across the cabinet, with agenda-bound run/retry/approve actions. Depends on the TODO-keyword registration bead.

title: Fix ogent-cabinet-agenda scope evaporating on agenda refresh
description: P2 (verified org-mode finding). ogent-cabinet-agenda sets a scoped agenda, but r/g refresh rebuilds over the user's global org-agenda-files, losing the cabinet scope. Bind the scope so refresh preserves it.
close_reason: Agenda scope sticky: ogent-cabinet-agenda sets buffer-local org-agenda-files in the agenda buffer so org-agenda-redo (r/g) keeps cabinet scope; ogent-cabinet-with-agenda-files macro retained

title: Ship org-capture templates for the companion/notes so they are reachable from any buffer
description: P3 (opportunity). org-capture is used internally (26 sites) but no user-facing templates exist to drop a prompt/note into the cabinet from anywhere. Provide capture templates.

title: Add an org-ql adapter for Cabinet search and saved views over OGENT_* drawers
description: P3 (opportunity). Cabinet search hand-rolls queries; org-ql over the OGENT_* property drawers would give composable saved views. org-ql call sites today: 0.

title: Add an ox export backend/filters so agent conversations are shareable documents
description: P3 (opportunity). Conversations are org subtrees; an ox backend (or export filters) would export a run as a clean shareable doc (md/html). Builds on existing ox- usage.

title: Fix detail window re-splitting into duplicate sliver on refresh/follow-link
close_reason: Split render (pure content) from display (window mgmt); reuse visible window, refresh never re-splits; follow-link captures buffer context; 5 regression tests

title: Add GPT-5.6/Fable-5 models, model roles, and oh-my-pi style picker
close_reason: Merged to master in PR #6 (merge commit 31a5815); CI green

comments.author: jake
comments.text: Implementation complete and under review in PR #6 (https://github.com/ryjm/ogent/pull/6); CI green on all four jobs. Close when the PR merges to master.

title: Unwrap br list --json pagination wrapper at the bd-list boundary
description: Live smoke in the running Emacs: br's list --json returns (:issues ... :total ...) but every ogent-issues-bd-list consumer (issue board, deps graph, new agenda projection) expects a bare issue list — bd classic returned a bare array. Board rendered nothing; agenda projected an empty file. Fixed by normalizing in ogent-issues-bd-list (both cache and fresh paths) via ogent-issues-bd--issue-list, mirroring the existing --coerce-single-issue precedent.
close_reason: Normalized in ogent-issues-bd-list for cached and fresh paths; helper accepts both br wrapper and bd bare-array shapes; 2 new tests; verified live in the running Emacs (agenda projects 32 issues, board data path restored)

title: merge=beads git driver invokes legacy 'bd merge' and silently drops issues
description: Repro: merging origin/master into a branch where both sides changed .beads/issues.jsonl produced a 104-line result missing 4 upstream issues (ogent-4c2, ogent-kid, ogent-pag, ogent-t3b); expected union was 108. .gitattributes maps .beads/issues.jsonl to merge=beads, and .git config defines merge.beads.driver = 'bd merge %A %O %A %B' — the legacy bd binary post-migration; the failure is silent (git keeps ours). Recovery that worked: git show BASE/THEIRS > .beads/beads.base.jsonl / .beads/issues.jsonl, then 'br sync --merge' (three-way base+DB+JSONL, verified 108 issues). Fix: repoint the driver at a br-based merge or drop the attribute so conflicts surface.

title: Interface coherence & build hygiene
description: Emacs-leg structural debt: duplicated interaction paradigms (hydra vs transient), three Evil integrations, keybinding-rule violations, checkdoc debt, and lint that never runs in CI.

title: Resolve the duplicate ogent-edit-menu definition (hydra defun vs transient prefix)
description: P1 (verified: defun at ogent-ui-hydra.el:224 and transient-define-prefix at ogent-edit.el:829). Whichever loads last wins, so the entry-point UI is load-order dependent. Keep the transient, drop the hydra wrapper (see the ogent-ui-hydra vestige bead).
close_reason: Kept the transient ogent-edit-menu (ogent-edit.el); deleted the hydra wrapper so the binding is no longer load-order dependent

title: Remove or fully wire the vestigial ogent-ui-hydra module
description: P2 (verified). ogent-ui-hydra.el has 7 hydras but contributes one clobbered command (edit-menu), one unbound command, and a dead defcustom; the project standardized on transients (9 transient prefixes). Remove the module or wire it intentionally. Pairs with the edit-menu dedupe bead.
close_reason: Deleted ogent-ui-hydra.el + its test suite; ported nav helpers as ogent-next/prev-response/request and ogent-navigate transient in ogent-ui.el; ogent-request-menu dropped (unbound duplicate of ogent-prompt-dispatch); registry/docs/declares updated; transient audit covers ogent-navigate

title: Consolidate the three parallel Evil display-buffer implementations
description: P2. After the keybinding rework one canonical helper (ogent-evil-display-mode-setup) exists, but legacy mirroring paths remain. Route every display buffer through the canonical setup and delete the rest.

title: Reconcile the C-c <letter> prefix bindings with ogent's own rule and the specs
description: P2 (verified). ogent binds C-c o and C-c d despite its rule 'never bind C-c <letter>'; the actual prefix is C-c . while specs/commentary still say C-c o. Pick the canonical prefix, fix the bindings, and align the docs.
close_reason: Review prefix moved off reserved C-c o to C-c ,; canonical prefix C-c . aligned across docs/specs/commentary; conformance test pins the contract. Implemented in a parallel session; verified and committed by this one.

title: Triage the 131 checkdoc diagnostics across 38 files
description: P2 (verified count via checkdoc with spellcheck disabled). The project's lint runner never surfaces these because checkdoc is gated behind missing ispell. Run checkdoc with spellcheck off in lint and clear the backlog (or ratchet).

title: Run lint in CI so the 'warnings gate merges' rule is actually enforced
description: P3. AGENTS.md says warnings gate merges, but no CI runs make lint and root lint.el is an orphan. Add a CI lint job (compile + check-declare at minimum) or wire lint.el.

title: Normalize ogent buffer naming to one convention
description: P3. Buffer names split across *ogent-...*, *Ogent ...* (Title Case), and capitalized variants. Pick one and normalize.

title: Stop mutating global state at require-time (notes hook, companion, issues load-path)
description: P3 (verified: ogent-issues.el add-to-list load-path at load and runtime; ogent-notes installs a gptel hook at require). Defer side effects to mode/setup functions so loading a file is pure.

title: Fix the package Homepage header pointing at the wrong GitHub repo
description: P3. The main package header's Homepage URL points to the wrong repo (the same wrong-account class as the CONTRIBUTING fix). Correct to the real remote.
close_reason: Already fixed at HEAD: ogent.el Homepage points at github.com/ryjm/ogent which matches origin remote

title: Re-anchor overlay-path edit application like the smerge path
description: ogent-edit-apply-as-overlay (and the overlay batch path) still applies at cached struct positions with no staleness check — same corruption class fixed for smerge in ogent-tdf.1. Port the ogent-edit--re-anchor helper into the overlay apply path. Inline-diff path is ogent-tdf.4, separate.

title: Excise Gas Town feature: delete gastown/convoy/refinery/agent-detail modules, tests, and docs
close_reason: Deleted gastown/convoy/refinery/agent-detail modules, 6 test suites, docs/gastown.md, and stale .elc artifacts (user-approved git rm)

title: Cabinet runner: ensure br worktree redirect so agents share the main beads DB
close_reason: Runner now writes .beads/redirect for linked-worktree workspaces (ogent-issues-bd-ensure-worktree-redirect, toggle ogent-cabinet-runner-ensure-beads-redirect); verified e2e with real git worktree + br claim visible from main checkout; 8 new tests

title: Tool safety & audit-trail integrity
description: Convergent finding across the architecture, gptel, and llm-capability audits: the live tool path does not honor ogent's own approval policy or proof ledger, and a parallel FSM/approval/render stack sits unwired. Highest-risk cluster in the audit.
close_reason: All children complete: confirm bridge to gptel (fk6.1, P0), ledger recording in live paths (fk6.2), orphaned FSM stack excised + ogent-ui canonical (fk6.3), MCP tools gated by default effects (fk6.4), legacy fallback re-execution fixed (fk6.5). The live tool stack is now the only stack, honors approval policy on every path, and records a complete audit trail.

title: Forward :confirm and an :effects-derived confirm flag to gptel-make-tool
description: P0 (verified). ogent-register-tools (ogent-models.el:261-272) forwards only :category/:async/:include to gptel-make-tool, silently dropping :confirm. The bash/write-file/edit-file tools declare :confirm t and :effects risk critical/high (ogent-tools.el:801-832), but the live path binds gptel-tools/gptel-use-tools (ogent-ui.el:1996-1997) so gptel executes them natively. Verified against the shipped gptel (build-30.1): gptel-make-tool HAS a confirm slot and gptel--display-tool-calls prompts on it, so the control exists and ogent simply fails to set it. Net effect: a critical-risk shell tool runs with zero confirmation. Fix: forward :confirm, and derive confirm=t from any :effects entry whose :risk is high/critical. Add a regression test asserting registered gptel tools carry confirm.
close_reason: ogent-register-tools now passes :confirm to gptel-make-tool, derived from explicit :confirm OR :effects meeting the approval threshold (ogent-tool-spec-confirm-p). Verified against gptel build-30.1 (confirm slot + gptel--display-tool-calls prompt). 5 new tests incl. the P0 regression; risky bash/write-file/edit-file now resolve confirm=t. Routing this through ogent's own effects-aware prompt instead of gptel's is tracked separately in ogent-fk6.3.

title: Record ogent-ledger tool start/finish in every live execution path
description: P1 (verified). Tool events never reach the proof ledger that architecture.org 'Proof Ledger' promises. The only ledger-recording code is in ogent-tool-fsm.el:114-149, which is unreachable from production (FSM called only from its tests). Live executors (ogent-ui--execute-tool ~ogent-ui.el:1888-1899, the async streaming path ~1788-1808, gptel-native handling) record nothing; requests are logged but every tool execution is omitted, breaking the replayable audit trail. Fix: record start/finish (with declared effects, result hash, error, duration) in all three live paths.
close_reason: Tool start/finish now recorded to the proof ledger in all live execution paths: ogent-ui--execute-tool (sync + async-fallback) records start+finish with result hash, error, duration, and declared effects; ogent-ui--execute-tool-async wraps the streaming callback to record finish on done/error; the gptel-native tool-result handler records a finish per result. Self-guards via ogent-ledger-enabled. 3 new tests.

title: Unify the tool approval/render stack: wire FSM+approval+render into the live callback or excise them
description: P2 (verified). Two divergent stacks coexist. Live: ogent-ui.el approval via allow-list + read-char-choice + session deny list (~137-272, 1855-1886) and :TOOL: drawers (~2138-2181). Orphaned: ogent-tool-fsm.el, ogent-tool-approval.el (transient + session cache), ogent-tool-render.el (:TOOL_CALL_* drawers, nav minor mode) are called only from their own tests, and the FSM string-response parser is a stub returning nil. Decide one stack: wire the FSM/approval/render modules into the live pipeline, or delete them and update architecture.org. Depends on the :confirm fix landing first.
close_reason: Excised the orphaned FSM tool stack (ogent-tool-fsm/approval/render + 3 test suites). The live ogent-ui stack is now canonical and the only stack. Rehomed the two debug commands that depended on it: ogent-debug-replay-tool runs through ogent-ui--execute-tool; ogent-debug-show-approval-status reports the live allow-list/deny-list. Wired ogent-ui--execute-tool to populate debug tool-history (was only ever fed by the orphaned FSM, so replay now works for the first time). Updated architecture.org + README/ROADMAP/cabinet-org-os/tools.org/debugging.org to describe the live stack. Retargeted ledger + debug tests. 2402 tests green, lint clean.

title: Declare :effects on MCP-registered tools so they pass through the approval policy
description: P2. MCP-registered tools carry no :effects, so the effects-based approval gate cannot classify them and they bypass the risk policy entirely. Attach conservative default effects (e.g. network/unknown/high) to MCP tools at registration, or require an explicit effects mapping.
close_reason: MCP-registered tools now declare default :effects (ogent-mcp-default-tool-effects = network/external/high, tunable). network is an approval-kind and high meets the risk threshold, so MCP tools are gated through ogent approval and forwarded a confirm flag to gptel instead of auto-executing. 1 new test.

title: Fix legacy tool fallback re-executing tool calls on every streamed chunk
description: P1 (gptel + llm-capability audits). On the legacy :tool-use fallback path the streaming callback re-runs already-executed tool calls on each chunk after a tool round, and closes the request when gptel delivers a (tool-result ...) payload, dropping the continuation. Guard executed calls by id and stop treating tool-result as request completion.
close_reason: Legacy :tool-use fallback now guards on the payload's eq-identity (new handled-tool-use request slot), so each tool round dispatches exactly once instead of re-executing on every streamed chunk. Separately, (tool-call ...)/(tool-result ...) conses are excluded from the completion-close branch so a tool result no longer drops the model's continuation. 2 new regression tests.

title: Strip gastown references from surviving files (keys, cabinet, issues, ogent.el, specs, README)
close_reason: Stripped all gastown references from keys, issues, cabinet, ops-style, transient-audit, specs, README, ROADMAP, CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and .claude settings hooks

title: Declare tool-use/media capabilities on shipped Anthropic models (fable-5 on old gptel)
close_reason: Shipped Anthropic registry entries now declare (media tool-use cache); ogent-models-apply-gptel-props unions onto the model symbol at send time so claude-fable-5 keeps tools on gptel <= 0.9.9.5. Regression tests added.

title: Fix lint: unknown-slot compile error and check-declare failures
close_reason: make lint green: removed bogus declare-function for magit-insert-section macro, dynamic slot access dodges EIEIO compile-time validation for runtime-only classes, org-roam/flycheck/evil declarations repointed at real defining files (FILEONLY for struct accessors/macro-generated defs)

title: ogent-ask: send whole buffer with point-area prioritized by default
description: Quick ask previously sent the bare question. Now ogent-ask--prompt includes the accessible buffer plus an excerpt of ogent-ask-focus-lines (default 5) lines on each side of point, labeled for priority; ogent-ask-include-buffer nil restores context-free behavior.
close_reason: Shipped: ogent-ask--prompt composes buffer + lines-around-point excerpt + question; defcustoms ogent-ask-include-buffer (t) and ogent-ask-focus-lines (5); 4 new tests; verified live in the running Emacs (model identified the exact cursor line from a 40-line buffer)

title: Fix Armory transient compatibility
close_reason: Fixed Armory Transient compatibility by expanding shared jump groups before Transient parses prefixes.

title: Edit pipeline correctness
description: inline-edits review flow has real correctness bugs (stale positions, unlogged resolutions, missing companion links) on top of being under-specified.

title: Re-anchor diff-review edits and add a staleness check before applying
description: P1 (verified). The diff review buffer applies edits at cached buffer positions with no check that the buffer is unchanged since the proposal, risking corrupt application. Re-locate by content and refuse on staleness.
close_reason: ogent-edit-apply-as-smerge now re-anchors: fast-path position+text check, unique-occurrence rescan updates struct positions, zero/multi matches refuse with status 'error + user-error; buffer never corrupted

title: Log resolutions and track pending edits from the diff-review accept/reject path
description: P2. Accept/reject in the diff review buffer bypasses ogent-edit-log resolution logging and pending-edit tracking, so the audit trail misses review outcomes. Route them through the logging API.
close_reason: Preview accept tracks edits via ogent-edit--track-edits in the source buffer; preview reject delq's from pending edits and fires ogent-edit-resolved-hook so ogent-edit-log records resolutions

title: Create and link a companion when ogent-request-edit proposes an edit
description: P2. ogent-request-edit never creates/links a companion, so its proposals are silently unlogged. Mirror the companion-linking the other edit entry points do.
close_reason: ogent-request-edit now creates/links the companion before the async response (when ogent-edit-log-to-companion); regression test added. Implemented in a parallel session; verified and committed by this one.

title: Make inline-diff display non-destructive until acceptance
description: P2. The inline-diff display method mutates the buffer before the user accepts, and reject cannot restore the original. Defer mutation to acceptance or snapshot for restore.
close_reason: inline-diff keeps per-region snapshots; reject-all restores original text; edits stay pending until accept/reject and resolution hooks fire either way. Implemented in a parallel session; verified and committed by this one.

title: Issue detail opens as a small bottom popup instead of a magit-style split
description: ogent-issues-detail-display-action defaulted to 'below (display-buffer-below-selected, 0.4) and the ACTION argument was silently overridden by catch-all display-buffer-alist rules like Doom's ^\* bottom popup (0.16 height) — the small horizontal popup users see. New default 'right: side-by-side split via display-buffer-overriding-action (outranks the alist); 'default option defers to user display-buffer rules.
close_reason: Default now 'right: side-by-side 50/50 split selected like magit, applied via display-buffer-overriding-action so Doom's ^\* popup catch-all cannot reroute it; 'below/'other-window kept, new 'default defers to user rules; 2 new + 1 updated tests; verified live in the running daemon (RET on ogent-cnw -> full-height right split, q collapses back)

title: LLM capability: meet the current frontier via gptel
description: ogent's gptel-based architecture leaves frontier capabilities on the table: prompt caching, multi-turn/compaction, structured outputs, a real eval loop, deeper MCP, and native agentic loops. Opportunity cluster from the llm-capability audit.

title: Adopt gptel prompt caching and per-model request parameters
description: P1 (opportunity). The installed gptel supports prompt caching and per-request params; ogent uses neither. Cache stable prefixes (system prompt, pinned context) and thread per-model params (effort/thinking) from the registry. Builds on ogent-gptel.el and ogent-models.el.
close_reason: Registry entries support :request-params/:capabilities copied onto the interned gptel model symbol per send (ogent-models-apply-gptel-props, capabilities unioned); shipped Anthropic entries declare (cache); ogent-gptel-cache (default t) bound to gptel-cache in ogent-ui--send-request; docs in backends-and-presets.md

title: Send multi-turn conversation payloads and add session compaction
description: P1 (opportunity). Every ogent-request is effectively single-shot; the org transcript holds the history but it is never replayed to the model. Assemble prior turns from the response headlines into the request, and add compaction for long sessions. Builds on ogent-ui.el request assembly + ogent-session.el.
close_reason: Multi-turn: raw prompt persisted as OGENT_PROMPT property; ogent-ui--conversation-history walks completed exchanges (model-matched fan-out, empty turns skipped, summary fallback for legacy transcripts); compaction = deterministic oldest-pair eviction under ogent-multi-turn-token-budget (LLM-summarization compaction intentionally not built); gptel conversation-list PROMPT wired into send

title: Wire the analytics eval loop: record completions in production
description: P1 (verified: ogent-analytics-record-completion has zero non-defun callers). The analytics module exists but nothing ever records a completion in the live path, so rate-up/down and the dashboard operate on no data. Call record-completion from the request-finish path with real token usage and latency; only then is the rest of the analytics surface meaningful.
close_reason: Wired the analytics eval loop into the live request lifecycle: ogent-ui-register-request marks start, ogent-ui--append-response marks first-token, and ogent-ui--close-response records a completion (model/prompt/response, with TTFT + latency) on success only. Self-guards on ogent-analytics-enabled; wrapped in condition-case so analytics can never break request close. This was previously fully dark (setup never called, record-completion zero production callers) - now record + manual rate (C-c . +/-) + dashboard operate on real data. 3 new tests.

title: Use gptel :schema structured outputs for edit / diagnostic-repair / codemap
description: P2 (opportunity). These pipelines parse free-form model text today. gptel-request :schema gives validated structured output, removing brittle parsing. Start with the edit pipeline (ogent-edit-parse.el) where malformed output is most costly.

title: Deepen the MCP client (protocol version, transports, prompts, resources)
description: P2 (opportunity). ogent-mcp.el pins protocol 2024-11-05, is stdio-only, and supports neither prompts nor resources. Bump the protocol, add HTTP transport, and surface MCP prompts/resources. Pairs with the MCP :effects bead in the tools epic.

title: Add a native gptel agentic runner for Cabinet agents alongside the CLI adapters
description: P2 (opportunity). Cabinet agents only run by shelling out to external CLIs (codex/claude/etc.). A native gptel agentic loop (tool-use + iteration in-process) would give a zero-dependency agent that shares ogent's approval/ledger/analytics. Builds on ogent-cabinet-runner.el + ogent-tools.el; depends conceptually on the tool-safety epic.

title: Add automatic model failover and retry to provider-fallback
description: P3 (opportunity). ogent-provider-fallback.el stops at an interactive login prompt with no automatic failover or retry/backoff. Add headless failover across registry models on transient errors.

title: Spec freshness: reconcile specs the code outgrew
description: Several specs now describe retired designs (streaming-into-src-blocks, smerge-only edits, gptel transport mechanics) or were never ogent-specific at all (the style guide is an imported handbook). These are spec-stale: change the spec, not the code. A spec that lies is worse than no spec.

title: Replace the imported alphapapa handbook masquerading as the style guide
description: P1 (verified by length+content). specs/style-guide.org (4,066 lines) is a near-verbatim copy of alphapapa's Emacs Package Dev Handbook with zero ogent-specific conventions. It cannot serve as the audit yardstick AGENTS.md treats it as. Replace with a concise ogent style doc capturing the conventions the code actually follows (;;; section headers, headerless sub-files, plists-over-alists, -- private convention, ogent- prefix, defgroup-per-module, autoload-on-entry-points) and link out to the handbook as reference.
close_reason: Replaced 4067-line imported alphapapa handbook with ~154-line ogent-specific style guide; every rule cites a verified file:line example; handbook attribution preserved in References

title: Rewrite architecture.org streaming / responses-as-data sections to match shipped design
description: P2 (spec-stale, verified). architecture.org 'Responses as Data' and 'Streaming & Error Handling' describe model output landing in src blocks closed by streaming callbacks. The shipped design writes prompt+context into a closed src block and streams the response as Org text under a '*** Response (model)' headline (ogent-ui.el:1270-1315, 1411-1477). The code is deliberate and tested; rewrite the spec. Also fix the Minor Mode Contract section claiming C-c o (actual prefix is C-c .).

title: Add OAuth / resilience / provider-fallback coverage to the gptel specs
description: P2 (spec-stale). specs/gptel-integration.org and gptel/overview.org predate the shipped auth/resilience layer: Anthropic + Codex OAuth flows, credential reuse, provider fallback. The Request Flow / Async sections also describe transport mechanics the code outgrew. Document what shipped.

title: Update inline-edits.org to the shipped multi-method edit pipeline
description: P2 (spec-stale). The spec describes a smerge-only pipeline; the code ships three display methods (smerge/overlay/inline-diff), a diff preview/review buffer, and quick-edit / diagnostic-repair / AI-speed-edit request flows the spec never mentions. The keybinding table is also pre-registry. Refresh.

title: Reconcile cabinet-parity.org promises that never shipped (org-roam/org-attach/sidebar)
description: P3 (spec-stale). cabinet-parity.org still mandates org-roam mentions, org-attach attachments, and sidebar/command buffers that were never built, and a Phase 0 fixture layout/test-file naming the suite ignored. Either commit to them (link to the org-trifecta epic where they overlap) or strike them from the spec.

title: De-monolithize oversized source modules (façade pattern)
description: Split the 4 over-threshold ogent source files into same-directory siblings behind a filename-preserving façade, strictly isomorphic (tests/surface/autoload-target/byte-compile preserved). Plan of record: ogent__demonolith_workspace/phase8_decomposition_plan.md. ogent-cabinet.el is a B11 justified monolith (leave-alone).
notes: Authoritative plan in-repo: specs/modularity/decomposition-plan.md (+ runbook.md). Full analysis (census, per-file seam findings, Phase-3 reader surfaces, baselines, fresh-eyes audit) in sibling workspace ../ogent__demonolith_workspace/. Each child bead is self-contained (winner design + requires/declares + autoload handling + fresh-eyes corrections inline).
close_reason: Executed on branch demonolith/modules; gate green (make test 2583/2576/0/7, byte-compile + check-declare clean).
